alertDisplayName: Unusual number of failed sign-in attempts
Description:
A relatively high number of failed sign-in attempts were observed during a short period. This activity can indicate an attempt to brute-force credentials.

For more information, read the IoT devices and Linux-based systems targeted by OpenSSH trojan campaign Threat Analytics report: https://security.microsoft.com/threatanalytics3/59e6e47e-9992-4d3c-b46e-d5cd825d89c6/analystreport?

Brute force attempt for user 'Account: LinuxMDC\user  - 4294967295'
remediationSteps: ["1. Make sure the machine is completely updated and all your software has the latest patch.","2. Contact your incident response team.","3. Install and run Microsoft’s Malicious Software Removal Tool (see https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/malicious-software-removal-tool-details.aspx).","4. Run Microsoft’s Autoruns utility and try to identify unknown applications that are configured to run at login (see https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb963902.aspx).","5. Run Process Explorer and try to identify unknown running processes (see https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb896653.aspx)."]
